description: Colorectal adenocarcinoma. RAS pathway status governs anti-EGFR eligibility; MSI-high status supports immunotherapy.

interactionRules.name: KRAS + NRAS co-mutation strengthens anti-EGFR resistance
interactionRules.description: Concurrent activating mutations in KRAS and NRAS represent dual RAS pathway activation; anti-EGFR therapy is essentially excluded.

effects.reason: Dual RAS activation makes anti-EGFR resistance prediction near-certain.

interactionRules.name: MSI-high with KRAS mutation supports immunotherapy over anti-EGFR
interactionRules.description: Even when anti-EGFR is excluded by RAS status, MSI-high tumors retain strong immunotherapy candidacy.

effects.reason: MSI-high status is a class 1 immunotherapy biomarker; co-occurring RAS mutations do not negate it.
